1.description: (Color online) Example of a Hypergraph G with n = 9 and m = 4 Note . Here, N F = { 1 , … , 9 } and E = { e blue , e red , e green , e black } with e blue = { 1 , 3 , 6 , 8 } , e red = { 1 , 3 , 6 , 9 } , e green = { 2 , 5 , 7 , 9 } , e black = { 2 , 4 , 7 , 9 } .

2.description: (Color online) Representing Assembly Networks and Power Dynamics Using Our Model Notes . (a) Assembly network. (b) Additional hyperedge for power dynamics.

3.description: (Color online) The Supply Chain Network from Figure 1 , as well as the Associated Auxiliary Graph G ′ Notes . (a) Hypergraph G . (b) Auxiliary graph G ′ .

4.description: (Color online) Equivalence Between the SCTM and LTM Activation Processes Notes . The superscript represents the period in which a node becomes active. (a) The SCTM activation process. (b) The LTM activation process.

5.description: Tree Decomposition of the Auxiliary Graph in Figure 3(b) Note . Each bag in the tree corresponds to a set of nodes from the auxiliary graph.

6.description: Histogram of ω ′ n + m for 657 Randomly Generated Supply Chain Networks Based on Willems (2008)

7.description: (Color online) Illustration of Jaccard Clustering Notes . (a) Higher Jaccard clustering. (b) Lower Jaccard clustering.

8.description: (Color online) Relationship Between Jaccard Clustering and the Seed Set Size for Different Data Sets Notes . Colors vary with the average length of supply chains in the networks, from two (light) to eight (dark). (a) Willems (2008) . (b) Random. (c) Modular.

9.description: (Color online) Illustration of Modularity Notes . The parameters are w i = c i = r i j = 1 and θ j = 3 for all i ∈ N F , j ∈ N S C . (a) High modularity. (b) Intermediate modularity. (c) Low modularity.

10.description: Starter Firm Percentage for Varying Numbers of Connecting Hyperedges on otherwise Identical Two-Part Networks, as described in Appendix E.1 in the E-Companion Notes . Each line corresponds to a different starting network. (a) m ¯ = 20 . (b) m ¯ = 30 .
